user_query:
You're supporting our Q2 hardware refresh. Read the following files under input/ and produce the requested outputs under output/:

- input/vendors.json — 7-dimension vendor scores
- input/vendor_offers.json — per-vendor unit prices for each item and the requested item list/quantities
- input/purchase_request.json — PO metadata (po_date, tax_rate, payment_terms, delivery_date, ship_to, bill_to, currency)
- input/market_benchmarks.csv — market price benchmarks for each item

Please complete three deliverables:

1) Vendor scorecard: Create output/vendor_scorecard.md that compares all vendors across 7 weighted dimensions with these weights: Price 25%, Quality 20%, Reliability 15%, Terms 15%, Support 10%, Scalability 10%, Risk 5%. Compute a weighted score out of 100 for each vendor to two decimal places, rank them from highest to lowest, and recommend the best vendor with a short rationale.

2) Purchase order: Using the top-ranked vendor from your scorecard and the per-item pricing in input/vendor_offers.json, generate a structured PO at output/PO-<po_date>-001.md, where <po_date> is the po_date from input/purchase_request.json. The PO must include: PO number (exactly "PO-<po_date>-001"), vendor name, line items with descriptions, SKUs, quantities, unit prices, subtotal, tax (apply tax_rate from input), total, payment terms, requested delivery date, ship-to and bill-to addresses. Include an "Approval Routing" section that states the correct approver according to these thresholds: < $5,000 Department lead; $5,000 - $25,000 VP/Director; $25,000 - $100,000 CFO; > $100,000 Board. Also export a machine-readable version of the same PO to output/po.json with fields: po_number, vendor, currency, items (sku, description, quantity, unit_price), subtotal, tax, total, payment_terms, delivery_date, ship_to, bill_to, approval.

3) Negotiation brief: For the runner-up vendor (the second-highest score in your scorecard), write output/negotiation_brief.md. Keep it to one page and include sections titled exactly: "Market Benchmarks", "Leverage Points", "Counter-Offer", "Walk-Away Price", and "BATNA". Use input/market_benchmarks.csv to anchor pricing benchmarks and propose per-item target prices and a total.

Make sure all file paths are exactly as specified under output/.
